title: Umami Creamy German Mushroom Terrine
description: A light and creamy German-inspired dish featuring the deep umami flavor of mushrooms, set with gelatine leafs and steamed to perfection. This Jain-friendly recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a fancy dinner party.
instructions: Soak the gelatine leafs in cold water for 5 minutes.
In a large pan, sauté the sliced mushrooms in a little water until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the vegetable broth, non-dairy cream, nutritional yeast, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Remove the gelatine leafs from the water and add them to the pan, stirring to dissolve.
Strain the mixture into a clean bowl and let it cool to room temperature.
Pour the cooled mixture into a terrine mold or a loaf pan lined with plastic wrap.
Refrigerate the terrine for at least 4 hours, or until set.
To serve, unmold the terrine and slice it into thick pieces.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve chilled.
